comment: 28yo male with strong family pedigree suggestive of FAMMM / CDKN2A hereditary predisposition: father diagnosed cutaneous melanoma age 32 (Clark IV, BRAF V600E), paternal grandfather diagnosed pancreatic adenocarcinoma age 55. Personal exam shows ~30 atypical nevi on trunk + extremities (not yet ≥50 threshold but clinically suspicious dysplastic-nevus phenotype). No personal cancer diagnosis. Engine fires RF-FAMMM-FAMILY-HISTORY-SUSPICION via family melanoma + pancreatic cluster branch and routes to PreventionPlan: standard = pre-test counseling + germline CDKN2A panel testing; alternative = empirical FAMMM-style surveillance (annual full-body skin exam + total body photography + EUS/MRI pancreatic screening from age 40 per CAPS Consortium) if patient declines testing.
